data.title: Crypto prices drop due to strong economic data, Bitcoin at $109,768 (-3.11%), Ethereum at $3,941.99 (-4.41%). Dogecoin also bleeds at $0.2271 (-5.31%). Over $1 billion liquidated in 24 hours. Long liquidations account for $1.08 billion. Bitcoin volume up 55%. Ethereum hits under $4,000 for first time in six weeks. Fear sentiment grows in the crypto market.
data.description: Cryptocurrencies, like Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, Solana, and Dogecoin, experienced significant drops in value due to strong economic data impacting expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut. Bitcoin fell by 3.11% to $109,768, while Ethereum declined by 4.41% to $3,941.99. XRP dropped 4.83% to $2.77, Solana decreased by 6.60% to $196.94, and Dogecoin went down by 5.31% to $0.2271. The cryptocurrency market saw over $1 billion in liquidations in the last 24 hours, signaling high selling pressure, with long liquidations accounting for a significant portion. Bitcoin's trading volume surged by 55%, while Ethereum's trading volume doubled in the same period, falling below $4,000 for the first time in six weeks. Additionally, Bitcoin's open interest decreased by 2.59%, despite a rise in the number of Binance traders betting on its price increase. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index indicated an increased "Fear" sentiment in the cryptocurrency market.
Russia’s fuel export cuts push oil toward largest weekly rise in three months.
Report: EU proposes 25%-50% tariffs on Chinese steel and associated products.

data.title: Kirin develops technology boosting heat and drought tolerance in hop seedlings for sustainable beer ingredient supply.
data.description: Japanese company Kirin Holdings Company, Limited (Kirin) has recently unveiled a new seedling cultivation technology aimed at boosting heat and drought tolerance in hops. This innovation, developed by Kirin, is significant due to the increasing issues surrounding reduced yield and quality of hops caused by extreme weather conditions. By enhancing acquired tolerance in hops, this method ensures the plants become more resilient without sacrificing flavor quality. The technology, focused on improving heat and drought resistance in hops, is anticipated to play a crucial role in ensuring a consistent and sustainable supply of key beer ingredients. Kirin's initiative, which involves deploying this advanced cultivation technique, is aimed at combatting the challenges faced by the brewing industry.

data.title: CenterPoint Energy declares $0.2200 dividend per share, payable on Dec 11, 2025, to shareholders of record.
data.description: On September 25, 2025, in Houston, CenterPoint Energy, Inc. (NYSE: CNP) announced that their Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.2200 per share for their Common Stock. This dividend is set to be paid on December 11, 2025, to shareholders holding stock as of the record date.
